title: For Loops Over Text
summary: Loop through a string one character at a time.

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to For Loops
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In Python, a for loop allows you to iterate over a sequence, such as a string. For example, if you have the string `"hello"`, you can loop through each character like this:

```python
for char in "hello":
    print(char)
```

This code will print each character in the string on a new line. Try it yourself by changing the string to something else!

sketchBlocks.title: Counting Characters in a String
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
You can also use a for loop to count the number of characters in a string. Here's how:

```python
count = 0
for char in "hello":
    count += 1
print(count)
```

This code initializes a counter and increments it for each character in the string. The final count will be printed. Your turn: Modify the string and see how the count changes!

quizDraft.title: Print Each Character
quizDraft.prompt: Write a program that prints each character of the string 'Python' on a new line.

quizDraft.title: Complete the Loop
quizDraft.prompt:
Fill in the blank to complete the for loop that counts characters in a string:

```python
count = 0
for char in "____":
    count += 1
print(count)
```

quizDraft.title: Understanding For Loops
quizDraft.prompt:
What does the following code output?

```python
for char in "abc":
    print(char)
```

quizDraft.title: Identify Loop Behavior
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following statements are true about for loops in Python? (Select all that apply)
options: They can only iterate over lists.
They can be used to count characters.

quizDraft.title: Count Characters in a String
quizDraft.prompt: Write a program that counts the number of vowels in the string 'hello world'.
quizDraft.starterCode:
string = 'hello world'
vowels = 'aeiou'
count = 0
# Your code here
quizDraft.solutionCode:
string = 'hello world'
vowels = 'aeiou'
count = 0
for char in string:
    if char in vowels:
        count += 1
print(count)
